topic: graphing inequalities on a number line
latex: Which graph represents all the solutions of the inequality $x < 7$ on the number line?
answer_latex: Number line with open circle at 7 and arrow pointing left (toward negative infinity)
keep_mc_reason: Question asks "Which graph represents..." requiring students to select from given graphical options

choices_latex.A: Number line with open circle at 7 and arrow pointing left (toward negative infinity)
choices_latex.B: Number line with closed circle at 7 and arrow pointing left (toward negative infinity)
choices_latex.C: Number line with open circles at 0 and 7, with line segment between them and arrows pointing both directions
choices_latex.D: Number line with open circles at -7 and 7, with line segment between them and arrows pointing both directions

diagram_notes: Four number lines labeled A-D, each showing marks at -7, 0, and 7. Option A: open circle at 7 with arrow extending left to negative infinity. Option B: closed/filled circle at 7 with arrow extending left to negative infinity. Option C: open circles at both 0 and 7 with a line segment between them and arrows on both ends. Option D: open circles at both -7 and 7 with a line segment between them and arrows on both ends.
